summary: trap-rollback-inventory now strips shell comment lines before mutating-like classification, so documentation-only setup examples do not count as rollback gaps.
why_flywheel_centric: The trap backlog drives Flywheel's next work. Comment-only mutation examples in hooks and docs should not send agents to add fake traps to read-only scripts.

proof.summary: 11/0 assertions including a tracked operational shell fixture whose comments mention git commit and ln -s while executable code stays read-only.
9/0 assertions; trap-rollback-inventory remains wired as a receiver.
Syntax and ShellCheck are clean for the edited script and test.
Live inventory now reports with_trap=98, without_trap=264, mutating_like_scripts=362, declared_read_only_excluded_count=2, coverage_pct=27.07.
No whitespace errors and no active dependency cycles.
